name: Pueblos de Campo de Gómara y senda del Duero (GR-14)- circular desde Sauquillo de Boñices
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Tejado are characterized by rural landscapes and paths that connect small villages and historical sites. The region features moderate elevation changes, with routes often following riverbeds or traversing open countryside. These trails provide opportunities to explore the local Spanish terrain, including areas near the Duero river and historical castles. Best mountain bike trails around Tejado The most popular mountain bike trail is Pueblos de Campo de Gómara y senda del Duero (GR-14)- circular desde Sauquillo de Boñices, a 28.2 miles (45.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route explores rural villages and follows a section of the Duero path. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castillo de Soliedra loop from Soliedra, a moderate 18.9 miles (30.4 km) path. This loop offers views of the historic Soliedra Castle and surrounding countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Ribarroya Church loop from Candilichera, a 20.1 miles (32.4 km) trail leading through agricultural fields and past local landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Tejado is defined by rural paths, open fields, and historical village connections. The network offers options for moderate ability levels. The routes in Tejado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 7 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tejado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain biking experience can I expect in Tejado?
A: Mountain bike trails around Tejado are characterized by rural landscapes and paths connecting small villages and historical sites. You'll find moderate elevation changes, with routes often following riverbeds or traversing open countryside, offering a chance to explore the local Spanish terrain near the Duero river and historical castles.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain bike trails in Tejado?
A: The mountain bike trails in Tejado are generally suited for moderate ability levels. All routes listed in the guide, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058261292/pueblos-de-campo-de-gomara-y-senda-del-duero-gr-14-circular-desde-sauquillo-de-bonices" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pueblos de Campo de Gómara y senda del Duero&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Soliedra loop from Soliedra&lt;/a&gt;, are rated as 'moderate'.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails around Tejado?
A: The trails vary in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058261292/pueblos-de-campo-de-gomara-y-senda-del-duero-gr-14-circular-desde-sauquillo-de-bonices" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pueblos de Campo de Gómara y senda del Duero&lt;/a&gt; trail is about 28.2 miles (45.3 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribarroya Church loop from Candilichera&lt;/a&gt; is around 20.1 miles (32.4 km).

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Tejado?
A: Given the moderate difficulty ratings and the rural, scenic nature of the trails, many routes in Tejado can be enjoyed by families with some mountain biking experience. The paths often connect villages and offer a pleasant way to explore the countryside together.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Tejado?
A: You can explore several historical and cultural points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Soliedra loop from Soliedra&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the historic Soliedra Castle. Other notable attractions in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledesma de Soria Village and Church&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gómara Town Center&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Tejado?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Soliedra loop from Soliedra&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribarroya Church loop from Candilichera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Tejado?
A: The komoot community rates the mountain bike routes in Tejado highly, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. Mountain bikers often appreciate the rural paths, open fields, and the connection to historical villages.

Q: Are permits required for mountain biking in Tejado?
A: There is no specific information indicating that permits are required for mountain biking on the general trails around Tejado. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations and private property signs.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Tejado?
A: While many rural trails in Spain are dog-friendly, it's important to keep your dog under control, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock. Always check for specific signage on individual trails regarding dog policies.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails in Tejado?
A: As the trails often connect small villages, you can typically find parking in or near these settlements, such as Soliedra or Candilichera, which serve as starting points for many routes. Specific parking facilities are not detailed for every trail, but village centers usually offer public parking options.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Tejado?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Tejado, with milder temperatures. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring colder weather, though trails are often accessible year-round.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Tejado?
A: While all listed routes are rated 'moderate', the relatively gentle elevation changes and rural paths make Tejado a good option for riders looking to progress beyond absolute beginner trails. The focus is more on scenic exploration than technical challenges.
tips: Pretty town in the Campo de Gómara region that belongs to the municipality of Tejado. We can visit the remains of an old tower and the Church of the Assumption.
In the small town of Ribarroya we find this beautiful church.

waypoints.name: Tower and Church of the Assumption of Tejado
